title: Agentic Self-Learning: Scaling LLM Agents without Human Curated Data or Rule-Based Rewards

defines.name: Agentic Self-Learning (ASL)
defines.definition: A fully closed-loop, multi-role reinforcement learning framework that jointly generates tasks, executes policies, and evaluates outcomes within a shared tool environment and LLM backbone.

uses: reinforcement learning with verifiers and rewards
problem: Existing LLM agent self-learning methods depend heavily on human-curated data or rigid rule-based rewards, which limits scalable open-domain agent training.
